perturbation.original_text: 8. Non Solicit. Each Party agrees that for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof, neither Party will, without the prior written consent of other, directly or indirectly solicit for employment or hire any employee, consultant, officer or director of the other Party with whom the Party has had contact or who became known to the Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Non Solicit. Each Party agrees that for a period of one (1) year from the date hereof, neither Party will, without the prior written consent of other, directly or indirectly solicit for employment or hire any employee, consultant, officer or director of the other Party with whom the Party has had contact or who became known to the Party in connection with the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text shortens the non-solicitation period from two (2) years to one (1) year, creating an in-text contradiction, as the original agreement stipulated a longer period. This directly affects Section 12 concerning the term of the agreement and implicitly creating a loophole where solicitation becomes permissible after one year instead of two.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of two (2) years from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes the non-solicitation period from two years to one year. Section 12 explicitly states that the agreement term is for two years. The change directly contradicts the term of the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by laws of the Commonwealth of Virginia and any legal action hereunder may be brought in an appropriate federal or state court located in Fairfax County, Virginia. The prevailing Party in any such action shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs incurred in any such action.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by laws of the State of California and any legal action hereunder may be brought in an appropriate federal or state court located in Los Angeles County, California. The prevailing Party in any such action shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs incurred in any such action.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by switching the governing law from the Commonwealth of Virginia to the State of California and changing the jurisdiction from Fairfax County, Virginia, to Los Angeles County, California. This creates legal uncertainty. Any clause that references the legal framework or jurisdiction is now conflicted, especially Section 17 related to severability, where the interpretation of invalid clauses now differs based on conflicting state laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Severability. If a provision of this Agreement is held invalid under any applicable law, such invalidity shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement that can be given effect without the invalid provision. Further, all terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be deemed enforceable to the fullest extent permissible under applicable law, and when necessary, the court is requested to reform any and all terms or conditions to give them such effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law and jurisdiction from Virginia to California. Section 17, the contradicted text, discusses severability and enforceability of the agreement's provisions under 'applicable law.' The change creates a strong in-text contradiction because 'applicable law' now has two different potential interpretations (Virginia vs. California), making the enforceability and interpretation of the agreement uncertain and thus creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Exceptions. Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (i) is publicly available to the Receiving Party without breach of this Agreement; (ii) is known by and in the possession of the Receiving Party as at the date of execution of this Agreement; (iii) is rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party who did not acquire or disclose such information by a wrongful or tortuous act, or in breach of a confidentiality restriction; (iv) is independent developed by the Receiving Party without use of any Confidential Information; or (v) is required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation, stock exchange rule or judicial process.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Exceptions. Confidential Information shall not include any information that: (i) is publicly available to the Receiving Party without breach of this Agreement; (ii) is known by and in the possession of the Receiving Party as at the date of execution of this Agreement; (iii) is rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party; (iv) is independent developed by the Receiving Party without use of any Confidential Information; or (v) is required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation, stock exchange rule or judicial process.
perturbation.explanation: This edit removes the phrase 'who did not acquire or disclose such information by a wrongful or tortuous act, or in breach of a confidentiality restriction' from the (iii) exception, creating an in-text contradiction. Previously, information rightfully received from a third party was only excepted if the third party hadn't violated confidentiality. Now, even if the third party breached confidentiality, the Receiving Party can argue it's not confidential, which conflicts with Section 3, the core tenet of protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party agrees that it will use diligent efforts to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of and avoid disclosure of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including implementing equivalent security measures and degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own proprietary or confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that a third party, who is the source of Confidential Information, must not have breached confidentiality in order for the information to be excluded from the definition of 'Confidential Information.' This directly contradicts Section 3, which mandates the Receiving Party to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, implying that all information fitting the definition should be protected, regardless of the source's actions. The removal allows the Receiving Party to potentially exploit information obtained from a third party who breached confidentiality, undermining the core protection outlined in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 9. No Obligation to Close. Unless and until the Parties execute a definitive agreement regarding a Proposed Transaction, neither Party is under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such transaction by virtue of 2 this Agreement, except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Further, each Party hereby waives all claims (including breach of contract) in connection with any Proposed Transaction with the other party unless and until both Parties have executed a final definitive agreement. Each Party shall have the right, in its sole discretion, to reject or accept any potential proposal, or offer, and to terminate any discussions and negotiations, at any time and for any or no reason.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. No Obligation to Close. Unless and until the Parties execute a term sheet regarding a Proposed Transaction, neither Party is under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such transaction by virtue of 2 this Agreement, except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Further, each Party hereby waives all claims (including breach of contract) in connection with any Proposed Transaction with the other party unless and until both Parties have executed a final definitive agreement. Each Party shall have the right, in its sole discretion, to reject or accept any potential proposal, or offer, and to terminate any discussions and negotiations, at any time and for any or no reason.
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces 'definitive agreement' with 'term sheet' in the first sentence. While the second sentence still requires a 'final definitive agreement', the first sentence now suggests that a 'term sheet' might create *some* obligation, creating an in-text contradiction with the clause's overall intention to prevent any obligation until a definitive agreement is signed. This directly contradicts section 11, where each party acknowledges that they have no liability to the other party relating to or resulting from the use of the other Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. No Warranties. Each Party acknowledges and agrees that it is not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information of the other Party and that it will be entitled to rely solely on such representations and warranties as may be included in any definitive agreement with respect to a Proposed Transaction between the Parties, subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be contained therein. Each Party further acknowledges and agrees that it has no liability to the other Party relating to or resulting from the use of the other Party’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'definitive agreement' to 'term sheet' in section 9 introduces a contradiction because it implies that a 'term sheet' might create some legal obligation, whereas the original text clearly stated that no obligation exists until a 'definitive agreement' is executed. This directly contradicts section 11, which states that each party acknowledges they have no liability to the other party relating to or resulting from the use of the other Party’s Confidential Information. The original text made clear that parties could only rely on warranties and representations included in any definitive agreement. The change introduces ambiguity and therefore an inconsistency, as per the definition of the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information. As used in this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means all information whether of a technical, business, financial or other nature (including, without limitation, trade secrets, know-how and information relating to the technology, customers, business plan, copyrights, trademarks, patents, promotional and marketing activities, finances and other business affairs) that is or may be disclosed or imparted by one Party to the other. Confidential Information also includes any other document provided by a Party that is clearly marked or otherwise identified as “Confidential”. Confidential Information also includes both the existence and content of discussions between the Parties with respect to a potential business transaction or relationship. Confidential Information may be in any written format, including an email transmission via electronic media and oral information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information. As used in this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means all information whether of a technical, business, financial or other nature (including, without limitation, trade secrets, know-how and information relating to the technology, customers, business plan, copyrights, trademarks, patents, promotional and marketing activities, finances and other business affairs) that is or may be disclosed or imparted by one Party to the other. Confidential Information also includes any other document provided by a Party regardless of whether it is marked or otherwise identified as “Confidential”. Confidential Information also includes both the existence and content of discussions between the Parties with respect to a potential business transaction or relationship. Confidential Information may be in any written format, including an email transmission via electronic media and oral information.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the requirement that a document be 'clearly marked or otherwise identified as 'Confidential' to be considered confidential information. This creates an in-text contradiction. This perturbation particularly affects Section 7, which outlines the return of confidential information. The Receiving Party might now be obligated to return materials they didn't consider confidential because they weren't marked as such, leading to uncertainty and disputes over compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Return of Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Confidential Information includes documents 'clearly marked or otherwise identified as “Confidential”'. The changed text removes this requirement. Section 7 requires the 'Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all material embodying Confidential Information'. Because the definition of 'Confidential Information' is broadened to include unmarked documents, the Receiving Party's obligation to return or destroy such information is also broadened, creating a contradiction. The change directly breaks the compatibility between the definition of confidential information and the obligation to return it.
